sourceMovie.title: Three Steps Above Heaven
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Three Steps Above Heaven (2010) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: After
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Three Steps Above Heaven clicked for you, After (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Love, Rosie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Three Steps Above Heaven clicked for you, Love, Rosie (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Three Steps Above Heaven?
faq.answer: Try After, Love, Rosie, After We Collided—each shares DNA with Three Steps Above Heaven while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Three Steps Above Heaven?
faq.answer: Three Steps Above Heaven (2010) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Three Steps Above Heaven part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Three Steps Above Heaven Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
